what_it_does: Provides a VersionProbe class that reads a running platform version by executing a VersionSource command with {binary} substituted by the executable path, stripping a configured suffix, and returning the first whitespace-delimited token of the output.
observations: The probe is stateless and data-driven; all platform specifics live in the VersionSource object.
It uses subprocess.run with stdout/stderr captured, text mode, and a timeout defaulting to 15s via getattr.
The command tokens have {binary} replaced with the passed executable string.
strip_suffix is removed from the end if present, otherwise any occurrence in the string is removed, and the result is whitespace-stripped.
The final output is reduced to the first whitespace-delimited token.
Empty command or empty output raises an exception rather than returning an empty version.
The module docstring frames this as LEVEL 1 and platform-agnostic, with zero platform-name literals.
standouts: Fail-loud design: empty command or no version output raises immediately.
Externalized platform knowledge: command and strip_suffix are data on VersionSource, not hard-coded literals.
Suffix stripping has an endswith-first, then replace fallback strategy.
conventions: Follows a structured module docstring with project/system/level metadata.
Uses getattr with sensible defaults for optional VersionSource attributes (timeout_s, strip_suffix).
Raises ValueError for programmer/config errors and RuntimeError for command failures.
concerns: A non-zero return code is ignored as long as stdout is non-empty.
The fallback replace(suffix, '') can remove the suffix from the middle of the version string, potentially altering semantics.
No validation that the substituted command tokens form a safe/expected argument list.
Taking only the first token may discard version information that appears after whitespace.
notes: A small, stateless adapter that turns a configurable VersionSource into a concrete version string via subprocess, with explicit fail-loud behavior.
questions: What is the exact schema/contract of the VersionSource object?
What does 'LEVEL 1 (platform-agnostic)' mean in this codebase?
Should a non-zero subprocess return code be treated as a probe failure even if stdout is non-empty?
What is the Mirror-Registry System and how does this probe feed into REFRESH section 4.4?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it probes a version by running a data-driven command and cleaning the output.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make version detection a generic, reusable operation that consumes platform-specific instructions from a configuration object rather than embedding platform names or command logic.
novelty: The insistence on zero platform-name literals and treating both the command and strip_suffix as data on VersionSource, making the probe itself platform-agnostic.
contribution: A reusable, stateless version-probe adapter that a fused system can keep to execute configurable version commands, clean their output, and surface failures explicitly instead of returning fabricated versions.
summary_for_embedding: VersionProbe is a stateless adapter that runs a configurable VersionSource command for an executable, strips a declared suffix, and returns the cleaned version string, failing loudly on missing configuration or empty output.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to provide a platform-agnostic, data-driven mechanism for reading the running version of a binary for freshness checks.

symbols.does: Defines a stateless class that probes a running platform version from a VersionSource.
symbols.description: VersionProbe is a small, data-driven class whose single responsibility is to execute a configured command, substitute the executable path for {binary}, strip a configured suffix, and return the cleaned version string. It embodies platform-agnostic version reading by keeping all platform specifics in the VersionSource object.

symbols.does: Runs the VersionSource command for the given executable and returns the cleaned version string.
symbols.description: The probe method builds the command list by replacing {binary} with the executable path, runs it with a configurable timeout, validates that output exists, strips the configured suffix (first from the end, then any occurrence), and returns the first whitespace-delimited token. It raises ValueError for an empty command and RuntimeError for empty output, enforcing a fail-loud contract.
